steps.title: Calculate the rate of water addition per second
steps.content:
The faucet produces one 0.03-milliliter drop every 3 seconds. To find the rate in milliliters per second, divide the drop size by the time interval:
$$\text{Rate} = \frac{0.03 \text{ mL}}{3 \text{ seconds}} = 0.01 \text{ mL per second}$$
After $t$ seconds, the total volume added from the faucet is $0.01t$ milliliters.

steps.title: Write the linear equation for total volume
steps.content:
The container starts with 24 milliliters. As water drips in, the total volume equals the initial amount plus the amount added over time:
$$v = 24 + 0.01t$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
